titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Edwards Amasa Park Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:21

did.abstract: The papers primarily document Park's work as Professor of Sacred Rhetoric (1836-1847) and Professor of Christian Theology (1847 to 1881) at Andover Theological Seminary, and as editor of Bibliotheca Sacra from 1844 to 1884.

chronitemset.event: Principal, classical schools, Weymouth & Braintree, MA
Received B.D. from Andover Thelogical Seminary
Colleague Pastor with Richard S. Storrs, First Church, Braintree, MA
Professor of Mental and Moral Philosophy; Hebrew Literature, Amherst College, MA
Professor of Sacred Rhetoric, Andover Theological Seminary
Professor of Christian Theology, Andover Theological Seminary

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School, 2017

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Edwards Amasa Park Papers (RG 291), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Edwards Amasa Park Papers, 1835-1899, document the professional work of Park. The collection is arranged according to the following categories: correspondence, sermons, lectures, notes on theological subjects, notes on readings, miscellaneous notes (fragments), student questions, newspaper clippings, and association items.

The correspondence is business and personal in nature, although much of the material relates to Park's work as editor of Bibliotheca Sacra. There is a name index to the correspondence available in the orignal finding aid for the collection, available at the repository.

The lecture notes are arranged according to order for a theological system prescribed by Park and described in document titled Syllabus of Lectures. This document is not written in Park's hand, but was almost certainly dictated by him. The document does contain revisions in Park's hand.

There is one document case of material which is arbitrarily assigned the category of association items. These items were found with unprocessed Park papers. The documents are not in his hand; and there is no readily apparent way to link them with other material in the collection. This group consists of a small number of letters and miscellaneous manuscripts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of letters and name index see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is primarily to Parks. For a full list of letters see original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton. Correspondents include David Fosdick, Jr., Leonard Bacon, R. S. Storrs, Increase N. Tarbox, Samuel Hopkins.

did.unittitle: Sermon delivered in the church at Lawrence South
Volume 1: Existence of God, Attributes of God, Authority of the Bible
Volume 2: Authority of the Bible (continued), Trinity
Volume 3: Government of God, Divine Decree, Will, Ability, Motive
I. Virtue/Depravity: I volume "Notes on ethics"; 1 folder notes on depravity

II. God: 1 volume "Theology for Bible classes of youthful students" ; 1 volume "Notes on the existence and attributes of God"; 1 volume "Nature of God"; 1 folder "Christian doctrine of God" (parts 3-4)
IV. 2 volumes "Trinity"; 2 folders: "Trinity", "Remarks on the difficulties in the way of apprehending the doctrine of the union of two consciousnesses in the God-man"
V. Government of God: 1. on causes, etc., 2. "Unity of the created system"

VIII. Atonement: 1 volume "The atonement"; folders: 1. papers on the union of Christ with his friends, 2. same as 1., 3. papers on the moral influence of the atonement, 4. "General reply to the misunderstandings of use, and Objections to the governmental theory of the atonement", 5. "Causes of Christ's sufferings", 6. 11 Atonement scenic", 7. Jonathan Edwards Jr. on the atonement, 8. Timothy Dwight on the atonement, 9. Schauffler on inspiration and atonement, 10. notes, 11. "The governmental theory of the atonement"
X. Sanctification: 1 volume miscellaneous notes on sanctification, 1 volume "Notes on faith"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged alphabetically by title; all are notebooks unless otherwise indicated

did.unittitle: 4. "Definitions on Theology", 5. depravity--"Report of the Deaf-Mutes with Remarks" (3 folders - Deals with issue of whether or not deaf-mutes can have religious knowledge), 6. "Dr. Willmer's Dogmatics and also, Thoughts on Philosophy and Theology, Realism and Nominalism", 7. "Formative Principles" 3 folders, 8. "Introduction to Theology11 /"Notes"; 9. "Miscellany Booklett II", 10. "Notes Principally on Space and Time"
11. "Qualifications of a Pastor", 12. "Remarks on Space &c. A few pages on Edwards", 13. "Thoughts on Philosophy and Theology", 14. "Thoughts on the Trinity", 15. "Trinity"
"Mr. Burquet's Classification" (1 volume), "Index to Miscellany" (2 volumes)
Bound volume of notes by student on Parks' lecture on Homiletics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. atonement, 2. the Bible, 3. christology, 4. doctrine/creeds, 5. eschatology, 6. evangelization, 7. evolution, science and theology, 8. God, 9. justification/sanctification, 10. liberalism/the "New Theology", 11. and 12. the Lord's Supper, 13. Lutheranism and Calvinism, 14. miscellaneous, 15. Park, 16. theology/philosophy, 17. unitarianism

did.unittitle: Undated letters from J. W. Edwards, and L. L. Robbins
Mrs. H. D. Ketchel to -- McKean; from Middlebury, VT
F. A. Reed to -- Draper; from Concord, [?];; Prof. Edw. Wiebe to A.S. Barnes & Co.; from Hamburg, Germany
A. M. Fairbairn to -- Duff; from Bradford, [?]
N. V. Hull to Warren F. Draper; from Alfred Center, NY
Yung Wing to -- Lane; from Avon, GT
to -- Witherow from Philadelphia, PA; Charles A. Aiken to William --; from Princeton, NJ
to W. H. Warren; from New Haven, CT
R. Henry Davis to Rev. W. DeLors; from Nugata, Japan

1. Abschnitt aus der Dogmatik von Prof; Julius Muller, 2. Clark, John C. History of alphabets. April, 1874, 3. Dr. Smyth's theory of the Atonement ... (document related to Andover Controversy?), 4. Dogmatik von C. R. F. Muller, 5. Eastman, Mary D. notebook of poetry

6. Extracts from a letter of Dr. Bushnell to Dr. Hawes, dated April 3, 1854, 7. Hermann Christian Karl Friedrich Hupfel, Doctor of Philosophy and Theology, at the University of Halle, 8. Moral van C. R. Tholuck, 9. notes

10. Park, Harrison G. extract from 22 Dec. 1820 sermon of D. Webster, Plymouth, Ma, dated 30 Jan. 1821, 11. Potio.in, Lemuel S. Nine Propositions on the Philosophy of the Atonement, 12. Syllabus for a System of Theology, ,13. Theologia Dogmatica. Halle: Semester October till March 1854-55. Dr. Julius Muller
